title: santalaceae
intro: The Santalaceae rank is a family of plants, specifically a group of flowering plants that are commonly known as the sandalwood or quasisand family. This family consists of about 50 genera and over 1,000 species, including the famous sandalwood tree, which is highly valued for its aromatic wood. Santalaceae plants are typically found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world, often as epiphytes or parasitic on other plants. They are known for their unique ability to absorb nutrients from their host plants, allowing them to thrive in environments with limited soil nutrients.
